The whole thing is built around the conspiracy cycle. To build a
conspiracy, there are 4 steps. You tell them a lie; you tell them it’s not
their fault; you tell them what to blame – that’s the real conspiracy
element, that’s the villain – and then you tell them the truth. That’s the
conspiracy cycle.
So, to get the big lie, just find your lie, the transition. Describe it. Tell
them it’s false. Tell them the results of believing this lie, and then the
transition.
What I would like to do is using this mind map in front of us, right now,
let’s take one or 2 people. Give us anything. Give us a lie, give us a
benefit. It doesn’t matter. And I’ll show you how you can apply this to
discover your lie, like if you don’t know what your lie is. If you do know

And this is why there is to blame. “All these shows have to display kids

a solution to this. You need…” I’m assuming that you offer a service.
And you don’t want to say, “You need my service.” What you want to
say is, “You need some technically savvier services. You services that
are smarter than your kids are with the internet.” Don’t say smarter
than your kids are. Again, that would be a little insulting. But, “Smarter
than your kids are with the internet. Because they are smart, but they
also curious.” And I would totally lean on the whole curiosity thing
there.
